artifacts.notes: DMM/supply/thermal readings used for this capture.

connectors.label: USB-C connector

pins.label: VBUS
pins.notes: USB-C VBUS logical power pin; confirm negotiated role/current before use.

pins.label: USB_DP
pins.notes: USB 2.0 D+ logical signal; do not splice without impedance-aware harness.

pins.label: USB_DM
pins.notes: USB 2.0 D- logical signal; do not splice without impedance-aware harness.

pins.label: CC1
pins.notes: USB-C configuration channel; identify orientation and role before use.

pins.label: CC2
pins.notes: USB-C configuration channel; identify orientation and role before use.

connectors.reference_note: Common USB logical pin reference seed; visual/reference only until measured.

measurements.notes: Unpowered resistance check between supply and ground.
Measure supply rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Record first-power current at a safe current limit.
Record no abnormal heating after first power.
Mark physical pin 1/orientation in a close-up photo before trusting any seeded pin label.
Unpowered check from each candidate supply pin to confirmed ground before first power.
measurements.target: USB-C connector (usb_c) supply-to-ground no-short: VBUS:VBUS vs GND:GND

measurements.notes: Measure supply pins under current limit and confirm source/sink direction before reuse.
measurements.target: USB-C connector (usb_c) supply voltage and polarity: VBUS:VBUS

measurements.notes: Confirm seeded ground pins to exposed ground, connector shell where applicable, and target common reference.
measurements.target: USB-C connector (usb_c) ground reference continuity: GND:GND

measurements.notes: Confirm D+/D- routing through protection/connector path; do not hand-wire high-speed USB casually.
measurements.target: USB-C connector (usb_c) USB data pair protection/path confirmation: VBUS:VBUS, GND:GND, D+:USB_DP, D-:USB_DM, CC1:CC1, CC2:CC2

measurements.notes: Treat high-speed pairs as reference-only unless connector, shield, and protection path are confirmed.
measurements.target: USB-C connector (usb_c) high-speed/shield reference check: D+:USB_DP, D-:USB_DM

visual_topology_measurement_queue.prompt: Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map USB-C connector: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on USB-C connector.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for USB-C connector before any external connection.
Continuity-test USB D+ and D- path from USB-C connector to the candidate USB bridge/protection network; keep polarity and pair orientation recorded.
Map UART header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Measure unpowered resistance/no-short between every candidate supply pin and ground on UART header.
Power through a current limit and record voltage, polarity, and logic domain for UART header before any external connection.
Identify TX, RX, GND, and VCC on UART header; measure idle logic high and run loopback or safe serial capture before target connection.
For CH340C USB serial bridge IC, measure continuity from USB pins to the USB connector and from UART pins to candidate headers before treating it as a USB serial adapter.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path CH340C USB serial bridge IC to USB-C connector; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.
Continuity-test the visual candidate path CH340C USB serial bridge IC to UART header; record both positive and negative results as topology_evidence.v1.

authority_closure.claim_boundary: This closure packet reports what measured evidence unlocks. It does not convert reference pinouts, visual geometry, or model text into physical repair authority.

next_action.prompt: Run and record the controlled bench outcome: first power, current, thermal, and function proof.

capture_summary.claim_boundary: Bench evidence covers only captured pins, observations, instruments, and attached artifacts.

validation: verify continuity and voltage under current limit
verify continuity and voltage under current limit

measurement_rows.notes: J1:4 power pin voltage and polarity verified.
Measured topology includes a shared ground reference.
Measured topology identifies low-voltage signal pins; idle state still belongs in first-power outcome.

pin_level_splice_contracts.claim_boundary: This contract is generated from measured topology only; it does not certify hidden board nets or external target compatibility.
do_not_connect_until: Do not join to an external target until voltage domain and shared ground are compatible.
Do not connect the power pin without current limiting and polarity confirmation.
pin_level_splice_contracts.label: fixture measured CH340C UART header
pin_actions: red wire: J1:4 -> current-limited supply positive (rail:3.3V).
black wire: J1:6 -> target common ground (GND).
pin_level_splice_contracts.scope: measured connector breakout and external splice only
validation_gates: Verify shared ground continuity before signals.
Verify supply voltage, polarity, and current limit before joining power.
Run UART loopback or known-safe serial capture before target connection.

wire_bom.requirement: Use DTR only after reset polarity and target compatibility are verified.
Cross RX to target TX after voltage compatibility is confirmed. Measured logic high: 3.3 V.
Cross TX to target RX after voltage compatibility is confirmed. Measured logic high: 3.3 V.
Connect only after voltage and polarity are verified.
Use CTS only after flow-control compatibility is verified.

required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground

resource_candidates.name: measured fixture measured CH340C UART header topology
resource_candidates.notes: Measured topology candidate; usable only inside the measured connector/pinout scope.

topology_authority.claim_boundary: Topology authority covers only explicit measured endpoints, nets, and connector pins.

topology_netlist_compilation.claim_boundary: Compiled netlists cover low-voltage measured rails, explicit load models, optional trace/regulator models, and voltage constraints only. Hidden nets, high-speed behavior, batteries, mains, and unmeasured loads remain outside this deterministic DC model.

load_envelope.pass_condition: Use measured load simulation and source-limit checks.
load_envelope.reason: Numeric load current is already modeled; no provisional envelope is needed.

topology_authority.claim_boundary: Topology authority covers only explicit measured endpoints, nets, and connector pins.

capture_integrity.claim_boundary: Integrity covers measurement packet auditability only; functional outcome and release manifest are separate gates.

claim_boundary: Session progress means the operator packet is becoming complete. It does not itself authorize power, splice, or repair; authority comes from measured topology closure and downstream outcome/release gates.

next_measurement.prompt: All template measurements are recorded; submit the packet to authority closure and continue outcome/release gates.
